summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding Android platform resources to MAUI projects.

arguments.Reason: Convenience overload. Use the overload with name and optional device ID instead.

summary.text: Adds an Android physical device resource to run the MAUI application on an Android device.

remarks.text: This method creates a new Android device platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the Android platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: The resource name will default to "{projectName}-android-device".

This will run the application on a physical Android device connected via USB/WiFi debugging. If only one device is attached, it will automatically use that device. If multiple devices are attached, use the overload with deviceId parameter to specify which device to use. Make sure an Android device is connected and visible via
adb devices
This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use
instead.

description.text: Add an Android device to a MAUI project:

members.name: AddAndroidDevice
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<MauiAndroidDeviceResource> MauiAndroidExtensions.AddAndroidDevice(this IResourceBuilder<MauiProjectResource> builder, string name)

arguments.Reason: Convenience overload. Use the overload with the optional device ID parameter instead.

summary.text: Adds an Android physical device resource to run the MAUI application on an Android device with a specific name.

remarks.text: This method creates a new Android device platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the Android platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: Multiple Android device resources can be added to the same MAUI project if needed, each with a unique name.

This will run the application on a physical Android device connected via USB/WiFi debugging. If only one device is attached, it will automatically use that device. If multiple devices are attached, use the overload with deviceId parameter to specify which device to use. Make sure an Android device is connected and visible via
adb devices
This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use
instead.

name.text: The name of the Android device resource.

description.text: Add multiple Android devices to a MAUI project:

members.name: AddAndroidDevice
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<MauiAndroidDeviceResource> MauiAndroidExtensions.AddAndroidDevice(this IResourceBuilder<MauiProjectResource> builder, string name, string? deviceId = null)

arguments.Description: Adds an Android device resource for a .NET MAUI project.

summary.text: Adds an Android physical device resource to run the MAUI application on an Android device with a specific name and device ID.

remarks.text: This method creates a new Android device platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the Android platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: Multiple Android device resources can be added to the same MAUI project if needed, each with a unique name.
This will run the application on a physical Android device connected via USB/WiFi debugging. Make sure an Android device is connected and visible via
adb devices
To target a specific device when multiple are attached, provide the device ID (e.g., "abc12345" or "192.168.1.100:5555" for WiFi debugging). Use
adb devices
to list available device IDs.

deviceId.text: Optional device ID to target a specific Android device. If not specified, uses the only attached device (requires exactly one device to be connected).

name.text: The name of the Android device resource.

description.text: Add multiple Android devices to a MAUI project:

arguments.Reason: Convenience overload. Use the overload with name and optional emulator ID instead.

summary.text: Adds an Android emulator resource to run the MAUI application on an Android emulator.

remarks.text: This method creates a new Android emulator platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the Android platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: The resource name will default to "{projectName}-android-emulator".
This will run the application on an Android emulator. Make sure you have created an Android Virtual Device (AVD) using Android Studio or
avdmanager
. The emulator should be running and visible via
adb devices
To target a specific emulator, use the overload that accepts an emulatorId parameter.
This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use
instead.

children.text: Multiple Android emulator resources can be added to the same MAUI project if needed, each with a unique name.
This will run the application on an Android emulator. Make sure you have created an Android Virtual Device (AVD) using Android Studio or

. The emulator should be running and visible via
adb devices
To target a specific emulator, use the overload that accepts an emulatorId parameter.
This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use
instead.

name.text: The name of the Android emulator resource.

members.name: AddAndroidEmulator
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<MauiAndroidEmulatorResource> MauiAndroidExtensions.AddAndroidEmulator(this IResourceBuilder<MauiProjectResource> builder, string name, string? emulatorId = null)

arguments.Description: Adds an Android emulator resource for a .NET MAUI project.

summary.text: Adds an Android emulator resource to run the MAUI application on an Android emulator with a specific name.

remarks.text: This method creates a new Android emulator platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the Android platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: Multiple Android emulator resources can be added to the same MAUI project if needed, each with a unique name.
This will run the application on an Android emulator. Make sure you have created an Android Virtual Device (AVD) using Android Studio or
avdmanager
. The emulator should be running and visible via
adb devices
To target a specific emulator, provide the emulator ID (e.g., "Pixel_5_API_33" or "emulator-5554"). Use
adb devices
to list available emulator IDs.

emulatorId.text: Optional emulator ID to target a specific Android emulator. If not specified, uses the currently running emulator or starts the default emulator.

name.text: The name of the Android emulator resource.

summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding iOS platform resources to MAUI projects.

members.name: AddiOSDevice
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<MauiiOSDeviceResource> MauiiOSExtensions.AddiOSDevice(this IResourceBuilder<MauiProjectResource> builder)

arguments.Reason: Convenience overload. Use the overload with name and optional device UDID instead.

summary.text: Adds an iOS physical device resource to run the MAUI application on an iOS device.

remarks.text: This method creates a new iOS device platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the iOS platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: The resource name will default to "{projectName}-ios-device".

This will run the application on a physical iOS device connected via USB. The device must be provisioned before deployment. For more information, see https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/ios/device-provisioning

If only one device is attached, it will automatically use that device. If multiple devices are connected, use the overload with deviceId parameter to specify which device to use by UDID. You can find the device UDID in Xcode under Window > Devices and Simulators > Devices tab.
This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use
instead.

description.text: Add an iOS device to a MAUI project:

members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<MauiiOSDeviceResource> MauiiOSExtensions.AddiOSDevice(this IResourceBuilder<MauiProjectResource> builder, string name)

arguments.Reason: Convenience overload. Use the overload with the optional device UDID parameter instead.

summary.text: Adds an iOS physical device resource to run the MAUI application on an iOS device with a specific name.

remarks.text: This method creates a new iOS device platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the iOS platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: Multiple iOS device resources can be added to the same MAUI project if needed, each with a unique name.

This will run the application on a physical iOS device connected via USB. The device must be provisioned before deployment. For more information, see https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/ios/device-provisioning

If only one device is attached, it will automatically use that device. If multiple devices are connected, use the overload with deviceId parameter to specify which device to use by UDID. You can find the device UDID in Xcode under Window > Devices and Simulators > Devices tab.
This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use
instead.

name.text: The name of the iOS device resource.

description.text: Add multiple iOS devices to a MAUI project:

members.name: AddiOSDevice
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<MauiiOSDeviceResource> MauiiOSExtensions.AddiOSDevice(this IResourceBuilder<MauiProjectResource> builder, string name, string? deviceId = null)

arguments.Description: Adds an iOS device resource for a .NET MAUI project.

summary.text: Adds an iOS physical device resource to run the MAUI application on an iOS device with a specific name and device UDID.

remarks.text: This method creates a new iOS device platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the iOS platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: Multiple iOS device resources can be added to the same MAUI project if needed, each with a unique name.

This will run the application on a physical iOS device connected via USB. The device must be provisioned before deployment. For more information, see https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/ios/device-provisioning

To target a specific device when multiple are connected, provide the device UDID. You can find the device UDID in Xcode under Window > Devices and Simulators > Devices tab, or right-click on the device and select "Copy Identifier".

deviceId.text: Optional device UDID to target a specific iOS device. If not specified, uses the only attached device (requires exactly one device to be connected).

name.text: The name of the iOS device resource.

description.text: Add multiple iOS devices to a MAUI project:

arguments.Reason: Convenience overload. Use the overload with name and optional simulator UDID instead.

summary.text: Adds an iOS simulator resource to run the MAUI application on an iOS simulator.

remarks.text: This method creates a new iOS simulator platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the iOS platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: The resource name will default to "{projectName}-ios-simulator".

This will run the application on the default iOS simulator. If no simulator is currently running, Xcode will launch the default simulator. To target a specific simulator, use the overload with simulatorId parameter.
This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use
instead.

description.text: Add an iOS simulator to a MAUI project:

members.name: AddiOSSimulator
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<MauiiOSSimulatorResource> MauiiOSExtensions.AddiOSSimulator(this IResourceBuilder<MauiProjectResource> builder, string name)

arguments.Reason: Convenience overload. Use the overload with the optional simulator UDID parameter instead.

summary.text: Adds an iOS simulator resource to run the MAUI application on an iOS simulator with a specific name.

remarks.text: This method creates a new iOS simulator platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the iOS platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: Multiple iOS simulator resources can be added to the same MAUI project if needed, each with a unique name.

This will run the application on the default iOS simulator. If no simulator is currently running, Xcode will launch the default simulator. To target a specific simulator, use the overload with simulatorId parameter.
This overload is not available in polyglot app hosts. Use
instead.

name.text: The name of the iOS simulator resource.

description.text: Add multiple iOS simulators to a MAUI project:

members.name: AddiOSSimulator
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<MauiiOSSimulatorResource> MauiiOSExtensions.AddiOSSimulator(this IResourceBuilder<MauiProjectResource> builder, string name, string? simulatorId = null)

arguments.Description: Adds an iOS simulator resource for a .NET MAUI project.

summary.text: Adds an iOS simulator resource to run the MAUI application on an iOS simulator with a specific name and simulator UDID.

remarks.text: This method creates a new iOS simulator platform resource that will run the MAUI application targeting the iOS platform using
dotnet run
. The resource does not auto-start and must be explicitly started from the dashboard by clicking the start button.

children.text: Multiple iOS simulator resources can be added to the same MAUI project if needed, each with a unique name.

To target a specific simulator, provide the simulator UDID. You can find simulator UDIDs in Xcode under Window > Devices and Simulators > Simulators tab, right-click on a simulator and select "Copy Identifier", or use the command: /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/usr/bin/simctl list

name.text: The name of the iOS simulator resource.

simulatorId.text: Optional simulator UDID to target a specific iOS simulator. If not specified, uses the default simulator.

description.text: Add multiple iOS simulators to a MAUI project:

children.text: You can add multiple Mac Catalyst device resources to a MAUI project by calling this method multiple times with different names.

summary.text: Provides extension methods for configuring OpenTelemetry endpoints for MAUI platform resources.

arguments.Description: Configures a .NET MAUI platform resource to send OpenTelemetry data through a development tunnel.

summary.text: Configures the MAUI platform resource to send OpenTelemetry data through an automatically created dev tunnel. This is the easiest option for most scenarios, as it handles tunnel creation, configuration, and endpoint injection automatically.

children.text: This method creates a dev tunnel automatically and configures the MAUI platform resource to route OTLP traffic through it. This is the recommended approach for most scenarios as it requires minimal configuration and works reliably across all mobile platforms.
Prerequisites:

description.text: Configure a MAUI Android device to automatically use a dev tunnel for telemetry:

summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding .NET MAUI projects to the application model.

members.name: AddMauiProject
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<MauiProjectResource> MauiProjectExtensions.AddMauiProject(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, string projectPath)

arguments.Description: Adds a .NET MAUI project to the application model.

summary.text: Adds a .NET MAUI project to the application model. This resource can be used to create platform-specific resources.

remarks.text: This method creates a parent MAUI project resource that serves as a container for platform-specific resources such as Windows, Android, iOS, and macOS. The actual platform instances are added using extension methods like
AddWindowsDevice

children.text: The MAUI project is not built immediately when the AppHost starts. Instead, builds are deferred until a platform-specific resource is started, allowing faster AppHost startup and enabling incremental builds during development.

builder.text: The builder for the distributed application.

name.text: The name of the resource.

projectPath.text: The path to the .NET MAUI project file (.csproj). This can be a relative or absolute path.

description.text: Add a MAUI project with Windows support:

summary.text: Marker interface for MAUI platform-specific resources (Windows, Android, iOS, Mac Catalyst).

remarks.text: This interface is used to identify resources that represent a specific platform instance of a MAUI application, allowing for common handling across all MAUI platforms. All MAUI platform resources have a parent

Represents a Mac Catalyst platform instance of a .NET MAUI project.

remarks.text: This resource represents a MAUI application running on the Mac Catalyst platform. The actual build and deployment happens when the resource is started, allowing for incremental builds during development without blocking AppHost startup.

to add this resource to a MAUI project.

Represents a .NET MAUI project resource in the distributed application model.

remarks.text: This resource serves as a parent for platform-specific MAUI resources (Windows, Android, iOS, macOS). Use extension methods like
AddWindowsDevice
or
AddMacCatalystDevice
to add platform-specific instances.

children.text: MAUI projects are built on-demand when the platform-specific resource is started, avoiding long AppHost startup times while still allowing incremental builds during development.

name.text: The name of the resource.

projectPath.text: The path to the .NET MAUI project file.
